data.content:
### Sandbox Operation Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.0.0/filesystem/page.mdx

Details on configuring sandbox operations, including timeouts and user settings.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Operation Configuration

### Description
This section describes configuration options for sandbox operations, such as setting timeouts and specifying the user for the operation.

### Timeout Configuration

#### `timeoutMs`

- **Type**: `number`
- **Optional**: Yes
- **Description**: Timeout for the watch operation in milliseconds. A value of `0` disables the timeout. Default is `60000` (60 seconds).

### User Configuration

#### `user`

- **Type**: `Username`
- **Optional**: Yes
- **Description**: User to use for the operation in the sandbox. This affects the resolution of relative paths and ownership of created filesystem objects.

```

--------------------------------

### Screen Resolution Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.9.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Describes the configuration for screen resolution, specifying its format and meaning.

```APIDOC
## Screen Resolution Configuration

### Description
This section details the `resolution` field, which specifies the screen resolution in pixels.

### Method
N/A (Configuration Field)

### Endpoint
N/A (Configuration Field)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **resolution** (array[number, number]) - Optional - The screen resolution in pixels, specified as [width, height].

### Request Example
```json
{
  "resolution": [1920, 1080]
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **resolution** (array[number, number]) - The screen resolution in pixels, specified as [width, height].

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "resolution": [1920, 1080]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Watch Operation Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/filesystem/page.mdx

Configuration options for the watch operation, including timeout and user settings.

```APIDOC
## Watch Operation Configuration

### Description
Configuration options for the watch operation, including timeout and user settings.

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **timeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Timeout for the watch operation in milliseconds. Pass `0` to disable the timeout. Defaults to `60000` (60 seconds).
- **user** (Username) - Optional - User to use for the operation in the sandbox. This affects the resolution of relative paths and ownership of the created filesystem objects.
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Watch Operation Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.0.3/filesystem/page.mdx

Defines configuration options for a sandbox watch operation, including timeout settings and user specifications.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Watch Operation Configuration

### Description
This section describes optional parameters for a sandbox watch operation.

### Method
N/A (Configuration parameters, not a direct endpoint)

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **timeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Timeout for the watch operation in milliseconds. `0` disables the timeout. Defaults to `60000` (60 seconds).
- **user** (Username) - Optional - The user to use for the operation in the sandbox. Affects path resolution and filesystem object ownership.
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Configuration Options

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.5/sandbox/page.mdx

This section describes the optional configuration parameters for a sandbox, including metadata, request timeout, and sandbox timeout.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Configuration

### Description
This section details the configurable options for a sandbox environment.

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **metadata** (Record<string, string>) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox. Defaults to an empty object `{}`.
- **requestTimeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Timeout for requests to the API in milliseconds. Defaults to `30000` (30 seconds).
- **timeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in milliseconds. The maximum allowed time for a sandbox is 24 hours (86,400,000ms) for Pro users and 1 hour (3,600,000ms) for Hobby users. Defaults to `300000` (5 minutes).

### Request Example
```json
{
  "metadata": {
    "userId": "user123",
    "environment": "development"
  },
  "requestTimeoutMs": 60000,
  "timeoutMs": 7200000
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - Indicates successful configuration update.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Sandbox configuration updated successfully."
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Watch Operation Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.12.0/filesystem/page.mdx

Configures the timeout and user for a sandbox watch operation.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Watch Operation Configuration

### Description
This section details the optional parameters for configuring a sandbox watch operation, including timeout settings and user specification.

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **timeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Timeout for the watch operation in milliseconds. Pass `0` to disable the timeout. Defaults to `60000` (60 seconds).
- **user** (Username) - Optional - The user to use for the operation in the sandbox. This affects the resolution of relative paths and ownership of created filesystem objects.

### Default Values
- **timeoutMs**: `60000`

```

--------------------------------

### Watch Operation Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.3/filesystem/page.mdx

Configuration options for watch operations, including timeout settings and user specification.

```APIDOC
## Watch Operation Configuration

### Description
Configuration options for watch operations, including timeout settings and user specification.

### Parameters

#### Request Body

- **timeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Timeout for the watch operation in milliseconds. Pass `0` to disable the timeout. Defaults to `60000`.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - User to use for the operation in the sandbox. This affects the resolution of relative paths and ownership of the created filesystem objects.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "timeoutMs": 30000,
  "user": "testuser"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - Indicates successful configuration.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Watch operation configured successfully."
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### E2B SDK Configuration Options

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.0/sandbox/page.mdx

This section details the available configuration options for the E2B SDK, which can be used to customize sandbox behavior and API interactions.

```APIDOC
## E2B SDK Configuration Options

This document outlines the configurable parameters for the E2B SDK.

### Properties

- **allowInternetAccess** (boolean) - Optional - Allows the sandbox to access the internet. Defaults to `true`.
- **apiKey** (string) - Optional - E2B API key for authentication. Defaults to the `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enables debug mode and connects to the local envd API server. Defaults to `E2B_DEBUG` environment variable or `false`.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to use for the API. Defaults to `E2B_DOMAIN` environment variable or `e2b.app`.
- **envs** (Record<string, string>) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox. Used when executing commands and code. Defaults to `{}`.
- **headers** (Record<string, string>) - Optional - Additional headers to send with the request.
- **logger** (Logger) - Optional - A logger object for logging messages (e.g., `console`).
- **metadata** (Record<string, string>) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox. Defaults to `{}`.
- **requestTimeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Timeout for requests to the API in milliseconds. Defaults to `60000` (60 seconds).
- **secure** (boolean) - Optional - Secures traffic to the sandbox controller with an auth token. Defaults to `false`.
- **timeoutMs** (number) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in milliseconds. Maximum is 24 hours for Pro users and 1 hour for Hobby users. Defaults to `300000` (5 minutes).
- **user** (Username) - Optional - The user to be used for file access.
- **useSignatureExpiration** (number) - Optional - Sets the expiration time for the signature in seconds.
```

--------------------------------

### E2B API Key Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Configures the E2B API key for authentication. It can be set directly or defaulted to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```typescript
optional apiKey: string;
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript - Sandbox Configuration Options

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.12.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Defines optional configuration parameters for E2B sandboxes. These include settings for internet access, API keys, debugging, domain, custom environment variables and metadata, request and sandbox timeouts, and security.

```typescript
optional allowInternetAccess: boolean;
optional apiKey: string;
optional debug: boolean;
optional domain: string;
optional envs: Record<string, string>;
optional headers: Record<string, string>;
optional logger: Logger;
optional metadata: Record<string, string>;
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;
optional secure: boolean;
optional timeoutMs: number;
```

--------------------------------

### Allow Internet Access Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Configures whether the sandbox is allowed to access the internet. This is a boolean option. Defaults to true.

```typescript
optional allowInternetAccess: boolean;

// Default: true
```

--------------------------------

### Optional Logger Configuration for E2B Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/sandbox/page.mdx

This snippet shows how to optionally configure a logger for the E2B sandbox. The logger can be any object implementing the `Logger` interface, such as the built-in console.

```typescript
optional logger: Logger;
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Docker Daemon for Insecure Registries (JSON)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/troubleshooting/templates/build-authentication-error/page.mdx

This configuration snippet allows Docker to send requests to a local proxy, which handles authentication. It is intended to be added to the Docker daemon's JSON configuration file.

```json
{
  "insecure-registries": ["host.docker.internal:49984"]
}
```

```json
{
  "builder": {
    "gc": {
      "defaultKeepStorage": "20GB",
      "enabled": true
    }
  },
  "features": {
    "buildkit": true
  },
  "insecure-registries": [
    "host.docker.internal:49984"
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "insecure-registries": ["localhost:49984"]
}
```
